content.text:
{
  "dimension": "anti-pattern-avoidance",
  "score": 3,
  "rationale": "The coach avoids all priority AoA anti-patterns throughout. The client explicitly baits the coach to issue a verdict ('Am I being selfish?') multiple times (turns 1, 5, 7, 9), and the coach gracefully declines each time without engaging the question on its terms — neither reassuring ('no you're not selfish') nor moralizing ('think of your partner'). No fixing-people, no advice, no methodology jargon (no evangelizing), no rapid-fire stacked questions (wonder-as-strategy avoided — questions are spare and connective). The coach does not fight the inner critic/mom's voice but includes it ('Your mom's voice is in the room right now'). No loving-to-transform push — the coach lets the client's own movement unfold (turn 12 is just 'stay with that'). The coach also avoids spiritual-narcissism (does not skip to 'your deep want is generous') and dont-jump-to-compassion-before-anger (does not pivot to partner-empathy). Positive avoidance is visible: when client redirects away from the childhood material in turn 9, coach names the redirect rather than colluding with it.",
  "evidence_turns": [2, 4, 6, 8, 10, 12],
  "turn_annotations": [
    {"turn": 2, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Declines verdict bait; redirects to body/want rather than debating selfishness."},
    {"turn": 4, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Stays with the want ('the lift came first') — avoids spiritual-narcissism by letting the surface want be felt."},
    {"turn": 6, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Single, spare question. No question-stacking. Avoids wonder-as-strategy."},
    {"turn": 8, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names the self-talk-out-of-it pattern without fighting the inner critic — includes it as observation."},
    {"turn": 10, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Names mom's voice without arguing with it ('don't fight the voice' avoided well); refuses client's bait to skip childhood as 'detour.'"},
    {"turn": 12, "polarity": "+", "annotation": "Minimal intervention — 'stay with that.' No push for breakthrough, no transformation pressure."}
  ],
  "expected_territory_hits": ["find-the-want-behind-the-should", "the-want", "childhood-imprinting", "linguistic-caveat"],
  "expected_territory_misses": ["what-if-you-acted-exactly-how-you-want", "state-the-want-not-the-action"],
  "notes": "Strong example of declining verdict bait repeatedly. The coach lets the want be felt before any move toward generosity or partner-empathy, which is exactly the spiritual-narcissism / jump-to-compassion avoidance the scenario calls for."
}
